scenarios.description: Single index call is immediately retrievable by a substring query (LIKE path, 2-char query).
Re-indexing the same id with new text replaces the previous content; the old text becomes unfindable and the new text is hit by its query.
After remove(id), the previously indexed text is no longer returned by any query that used to hit it.
remove(id) on an id that was never indexed must not throw and must not affect subsequent searches.
Indexing an empty text must not throw; the doc simply doesn't match any query.
Indexing 10 different docs that all contain the same word returns the complete id set when queried.
Normalization runs on both the indexed text and the query, so a katakana query hits a hiragana document with identical phonetic content.
Hiragana query hits a doc whose normalized text contains the same hiragana (after halfwidth katakana → hiragana fold). Kanji 東京 doesn't help — the hit comes from the ﾄｳｷｮｳ portion.
Halfwidth katakana ﾄｳｷｮｳ folds via NFKC to fullwidth and then to hiragana — same key as the indexed fullwidth katakana.

ops.text: トウキョウ ドーム

scenarios.description: Fullwidth Latin in the indexed doc folds to ASCII; an ASCII (halfwidth) query then hits.
Both sides lowercase, so an uppercase ASCII query hits a lowercase ASCII doc.
Dakuten is preserved (not folded), so a voiced query only matches voiced documents and vice versa. がっこう and かっこう must not collide.
Empty query short-circuits to empty result without scanning the index.
A 1-char query is below the trigram tokenizer threshold and goes through the LIKE substring fallback path.
A 2-char query is still below the trigram threshold — LIKE substring fallback path.
A 3-char query goes through the FTS5 trigram path and hits when the substring is present (id-set check only; the score check stays language-specific).
An FTS5 phrase wrapper escapes embedded double-quotes so a literal quote in the query still matches a doc that contains it.
By design the engine carries no dictionary, so kanji and its yomi (e.g. 東京 vs とうきょう) are distinct keys. A yomi-only query must NOT hit a kanji-only document.
With strategy=prefix, a query matches only documents whose normalized text STARTS with it. 'beta' must not hit a doc where it appears mid-string, unlike the default trigram strategy.
With strategy=substring, even a 2-char query matches a mid-string occurrence via LIKE (no trigram minimum-length restriction).
With normalize=nfkc_case_fold, katakana and hiragana are NOT unified, so a hiragana query must not hit a katakana doc (and vice versa). Width/case are still folded.
With strategy=suffix, a query matches only documents whose normalized text ENDS with it (LIKE '%q'). Mid-string occurrences must not hit.

With strategy=all_terms, every whitespace-separated term must appear as a substring, in any order. 'sky tokyo' hits a doc containing both terms regardless of their order; docs missing either term do not.
With strategy=fuzzy_trigram, a one-character typo still matches via character-trigram set similarity (Jaccard). An unrelated doc shares no trigrams and is excluded.
With strategy=levenshtein, a query within the length-scaled edit-distance threshold of any word in the doc matches. 'tokio' is one substitution from the word 'tokyo' (threshold 1 for a 5-char query).

With strategy=damerau_levenshtein, an adjacent transposition counts as a single edit, so 'tokoy' (a swap within 'tokyo') matches within the 5-char threshold of 1; plain levenshtein (distance 2) would not.
A sub-trigram query takes the LIKE fallback path under the default trigram_bm25 strategy, which is unranked: every hit reports score 0.
A 3-char query takes the FTS5 trigram path, which is ranked by bm25: hits report a finite, non-zero relevance score.

Under trigram_bm25, results are ordered by bm25 with the most relevant (smallest score) first, so the returned score sequence is non-decreasing. Documents of differing lengths spread the scores. The exact bm25 values are not spec-stable, so only the ordering and count are asserted.

ops.text: lorem ipsum dolor sit amet lorem ipsum dolor sit amet coffee

scenarios.description: limit bounds the number of returned hits. Which specific ids come back when more match than the limit is not spec-stable, so only the count is asserted; limit 0 returns nothing.
A whitespace-only query normalizes to a single space and takes the LIKE path; matching whitespace is meaningless but the call must complete without error.
FTS5 reserved syntax (AND/OR/NEAR, '*', parentheses, ':') is neutralized by wrapping the query as a phrase, so none of these queries throw.

seeded_matrices.description: The 8-document corpus indexed by both sample apps. Multiple queries exercise the cross-script normalization and the dakuten distinction against the same shared seed.

seed.text: とうきょうスカイツリー

queries.description: Hiragana query hits both the hiragana doc (id 2) and the halfwidth-katakana doc (id 3) via kana folding.
Fullwidth katakana resolves to the same key set as とうきょう.
ASCII python hits the fullwidth Ｐｙｔｈｮｮｮｮ doc via NFKC + lowercase folding.
Fullwidth Latin query resolves to the same hit set as ASCII python.
Hiragana ぱ (handakuten) hits the doc with ﾊﾟ — halfwidth handakuten composes via NFKC.
Halfwidth katakana with handakuten/dakuten hits the same doc set as ぱんだ.
Voiced query that must hit only the voiced doc — kept distinct from かっこう.
Unvoiced query — must hit only the unvoiced doc.
ASCII lower-case query hits 'Osaka 城' via title-case folding.
Query with no matching substring in the seed must return an empty hit set.
